summary.headline: Revenue closed $970K in June on the spring buying peak; genuine HIH softened seasonally to 77; funnel totals inflated by a lifecycle import.
summary.body: June's headline number is revenue: $970K closed won (34 deals, 82% district) — the strongest month this fiscal year and on-cycle for the spring–summer district buying peak. Two mega-deals drove it: Idea Public Schools ($641K) and Yonkers ($160K) together = 82% of the total. Genuine demand softened as expected entering summer: HIH at 77 (down from 101 in May, −24%) — seasonal, not a signal problem. MQL/SQL/Opp totals are inflated by a lifecycle import event (~95% OFFLINE-source, no product tagging); product-tagged funnel is flat vs May (IJ HIH 55, SQL 30). Web traffic pulled back to 28,599 sessions (from 39K in May — normal summer softening). Win rate context: 63% count / 83% dollar on close-date basis, but 13 of 20 losses are aged deals (backlog flush — not current selling).

signals.label: Lifecycle import inflated MQL/SQL (not genuine demand)
IJ genuine funnel flat vs May

kpis.label: MQL → SQL Conv.
kpis.sub: Week of Jul 13 · offline lifecycle still loading

weekly_signal.narrative: Week of Jul 13: 27 new MQLs, 23 SQLs, 23 Opps — raw velocity up WoW but MQL→SQL at 85% (23/27) is inflated by ongoing offline lifecycle migration, consistent with prior weeks. HIH at 5 new entries — deepest summer low of the season, expected. Closed-won July MTD: $704K across 44 deals, pacing well for a summer month. Open pipeline: 151 deals (110 district, 41 school). No competitor site signals this run — sites returned 403.
